purpose: Monitoring companion app for DENG Tool: Rejoin
changelog: Network errors are now clear and recoverable: a failed connection shows a host-named message such as 'Cannot reach tool.deng.my.id — check your internet/DNS connection and try again.' with a Retry button, instead of the previous cryptic 'Network error: UnknownHostException' that looked like the app was stuck loading forever.
Settings → About now shows the backend the app is built against ('API host: tool.deng.my.id') so connectivity issues can be diagnosed at a glance. No tokens or secrets are ever displayed.
Dashboard, Packages, Snapshot, and Settings all render a retry-capable error card on network failure and keep auto-retrying in the background — no more infinite spinner.

Backend now tolerates a not-yet-applied migration 010: the device heartbeat (last_seen_at) and the optional bridge diagnostics are written in separate updates, so a missing last_bridge_status column can never roll back the heartbeat and falsely mark devices Disconnected.
No functional change to the v1.0.4 connection-TTL, instant-settings-refresh, snapshot diagnostics, or 5-state package model — this release hardens connectivity and error handling on top of them.
